item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Latest technologies are affected from the longest range.

I recommend immediate reclassification to Keter and summary decommissioning by overwhelming lethal force applied via primitive technology, on the grounds that SCP-2184 constitutes an in-progress XK Class End of the World Scenario. It's just happening very slowly.

Since newer technology is affected from further away, and time is linear, and human technological progress is currently accelerating at an exponential rate, it follows that eventually SCP-2184 will affect the entire planet. Even space travel might be insufficient for humanity to escape the effects of SCP-2184, since the rate of technological progress is accelerating but the rate of travel through space has a fixed known upper-limit.

Furthermore, any new containment procedures developed by the Foundation are, by definition, novel modern technology circa whatever year the containment procedures were developed. This means SCP-2184 not only poses a long-term existential threat to all Foundation sites and facilities occupying the same physical universe as SCP-2184, but it is also currently attempting containment breaches of nearly all SCP-objects across all Foundation sites on Earth simultaneously. Again, it's just happening very slowly from our perspective.

The longer humanity coexists with SCP-2184, the more likely it is that all of humanity will be reduced to using primitive technology. Since the vast majority of the objects within our care cannot be contained using primitive technology, it follows that containment of SCP-2184 a non-starter.

SCP-2184 must be decommissioned. The question is not if, but when.

(Note: I'm not asking for removal of the article. This post is just an over-the-top in-universe way of suggesting an edit explaining that yes, left in containment, eventually this thing will eat Human Progress as a concept, and yes, the Foundation is aware of it and is working on a plan. We probably have at least a few decades to keep it contained while debating the ethics before it starts affecting Things Newer than Computers, but if nearby towns are mysteriously slow to adopt some new game-changing consumer technology, we'll know its area of effect has already exceeded current containment.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just liked the image of it Road Runner-ing across the pit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Generally pretty cool, though I feel as if something could be added to it if some sort of extremely specific technology was unaffected by the SCP. It could perhaps add a level of wonder to the whole ordeal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's nothing about how the thing is actually contained in the Special Containment Procedures. It's all about how staff are to be armed and the wall around the Site itself (which the SCP could go right through). I think that's a real shame because a description of how the Foundation tries to contain this thing with the limited means available would be the most interesting part of the article. The rest was somewhat bland. Except the part where it walked over the pit 'cause it was made by explosives. I liked that.

Reluctant -1, though I would happily upvote if you made some revisions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: (Is there a way to delete all my comments?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Significantly better than 1297, more interesting than 804, and includes the phrase "pit malfunctioned". + all the 1s.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No vote, seems good but I'm just not hooked.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's kind of why I'm iffy about it. It's too similar to the Ice-Age-Causing Mammoth. It's otherwise good though, so I didn't downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, it might be related, it's up to your headcanon. (I made this a steppe mammoth and not a woolly, because they were used in that skip.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this. I wonder if it's related to the Ice-Age-Causing Mammoth..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the idea of foundation agents forced to dress like cavemen to contain this thing, but a similar concept was already done with SCP-804. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice idea! I did not consider this, when I wrote the skip. Might edit it in later, if the page avoids deletion now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder, does the effect extend to modern languages? I'm going to imagine it does, just because I love the image of an MTF that communicates exclusively through grunting and pointing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually I wasn't aware of 1297 when I wrote this. I see that it's similar to it, but I hope it's unique enough to survive in the mainlist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Written well but ultimately also reminds me of SCP-1297, and the concept is played even more straight since the object doing the thing is a mammoth. There's no real cool story or background. You almost come close with the recovery log, specifically this bit:

SCP-2184 was discovered by Agent ████████ in ████ when he conducted a routine investigation to determine whether the mythology of the Siberian ████████-██████ tribe was based on the observation of anomalous objects or phenomena.

But ultimately, it just falls flat. I wouldn't mind it falling flat if it did it in an interesting way, but when it reminds me of another skip and nothing is really done with it to make it stand out, I can't do anything but downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It makes me think of SCP-1297 far too much, without actually doing anything interesting with the concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No vote for now. I don't know why, but something about it feels off

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: New SCP, hope you will enjoy it. Thanks to everyone who helped with the draft in the forum or in the chat.
